title: Tactics: Back Rank Mate
summary: The most common checkmate pattern - trap the enemy king on the back rank.
description: Back rank mate occurs when the enemy king is trapped on the back rank by its own pawns and a rook or queen delivers checkmate along the rank. This is one of the most frequent tactical patterns in chess, even at the highest levels.
objectives: Recognize when the back rank is vulnerable
Understand how pawns can trap a king
Learn to create back rank mates from seemingly quiet positions
plans: Target the back rank - line up rook or queen with the enemy king
Often need to remove or block the defender in front of the king
The side being mated often has no escape squares
commonMistakes: Don't forget about the king's escape squares - especially g7/f7 or b7/c7
Often need to eliminate the defending piece first
A pawn move can sometimes defend the back rank
completionSummary: You've learned to recognize and execute back rank mates, a fundamental tactical pattern.

steps.title: Open the game
steps.instruction: Play e4 to control the center.
steps.hint: Push the king pawn two squares.

steps.title: Queen captures
steps.instruction: Capture with the queen, developing with check.
steps.hint: Capture on d4 with the queen.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black develops the knight to c6.

steps.title: Queen to e5
steps.instruction: Move the queen to e5, attacking f7.

steps.title: Black defends
steps.instruction: Black develops the bishop to e7.

steps.title: Develop the knight
steps.instruction: Move Nc3, developing toward the center.

steps.title: Black develops
steps.instruction: Black develops the knight to f6.

steps.title: Rook to d1
steps.instruction: Move the rook to d1, connecting the rooks.

steps.title: Black castles
steps.coaching: Now Black's king is on g8. The rooks are connected but the king is somewhat exposed.

steps.title: Rook to d8
steps.instruction: Move the rook to d8, pressuring the d-file.

steps.title: Black defends
steps.instruction: Black moves the rook to e8, defending the back rank.
steps.coaching: The rook on e8 blocks the back rank temporarily. But we can change that...

steps.title: Capture on d8
steps.instruction: Capture on d8! This removes Black's defending rook.
steps.coaching: The queen on e5 attacks d8. The rook captures, winning the rook and clearing the d-file.
steps.hint: Capture on d8 with the rook!

steps.title: Queen recaptures
steps.coaching: Now the queen is on d8. But look at Black's back rank...

steps.title: The back rank mate
steps.instruction: Play Rd1#! The queen cannot capture (the king is in check from e5), and the back rank is exposed!
steps.coaching: CHECKMATE! The king cannot move (f8 is blocked by the pawn on f7), cannot capture the rook, and cannot block. Classic back rank mate!
